summary: Draft PR adding two Transmuter helper scripts and a deliberate source perturbation in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds to demonstrate the tool. The bot report showed two broken matches in main/melee/if/iftime, and reviewer feedback requested resubmission in a more complete state with clearer usage documentation. The PR was closed unmerged.

key_files.role: new helper script to generate a ctx file for a source TU, copy it to .ctx.c, derive the target object path, locate the Transmuter CLI, and print a suggested transmuter match invocation
new compile wrapper intended to replicate the mwcc_sjis ninja rule for a single preprocessed ctx-style source file
evidence_refs: raw_diff: comment says it replicates the mwcc_sjis ninja rule for a single preprocessed TU

key_files.role: demo target; declaration order in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds changed intentionally to break the function for Transmuter demonstration
evidence_refs: raw_diff: centiseconds declaration moved after seconds declaration
pr_body: author says they purposely break ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds to demo Transmuter

what_changed.text: Added tools/transmuter-prepare.sh, which validates a source path under src/, builds the corresponding build/GALE01/*.ctx with ninja, copies it to .ctx.c, derives build/GALE01/obj/*.o, and prints a transmuter match command.
Added tools/transmuter-compile.sh, a bash wrapper around sjiswrap.exe and mwcceppc.exe using MW_VERSION GC/1.2.5n and CFLAGS matching the Melee build context.
Changed ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds by moving the gm_8016AF0C centiseconds call after the gm_8016AEEC seconds call.
PR body documented a sample Transmuter command using --target, --function, --compiler, --cwd, --isolate, --no-reduce, --no-cleanup, concurrency, max iterations, timeout, and seed.
Automated report showed matched code decreased by 0.01% / 260 bytes and listed broken matches for ifTime_UpdateCountdown and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.

smart_moves.text: The demo isolated Transmuter setup into tooling scripts rather than requiring the command to be reconstructed manually each time.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/transmuter-prepare.sh prints Ready. To run the match

smart_moves.text: The prepare script derived build paths from the source path and checked for missing source and target object files before printing the Transmuter command.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: source must live under src/
raw_diff: target object not found; run ninja first

smart_moves.text: The compile wrapper selected wibo on Linux when available and otherwise fell back to wine, matching the stated intent to mirror configure.py behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Pick PE loader comment and WRAPPER selection

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not treat a tool demonstration that intentionally breaks a matched function as decomp progress; preserve regression evidence and reviewer disposition separately from reusable matching tactics.
evidence_refs: pr_body: I purposely break the function ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds to demo Transmuter
text_corpus: bot report lists broken matches
issue_comment: reviewer asked to resubmit in a more complete state

decomp_lessons.lesson: When introducing a matching-search tool workflow, the PR should explain usage clearly enough for reviewers; unclear or incomplete tool documentation can block acceptance even if scripts are present.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: Please resubmit this when it's in a more complete state and with a clearer explanation of its usage

naming_conventions.name: Transmuter helper script names
naming_conventions.convention: The PR used tools/transmuter-prepare.sh for ctx preparation and tools/transmuter-compile.sh for the compiler wrapper.

naming_conventions.name: ctx-derived build artifacts
naming_conventions.convention: The prepare script used BASE=build/GALE01/${SRC%.c}, CTX=$BASE.ctx, CTX_C=$BASE.ctx.c, and TARGET_O=build/GALE01/obj/${SRC#src/} with .o extension.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a generated preprocessed single-TU ctx source as Transmuter input, then copy it to .ctx.c so mwcc and Transmuter accept the extension.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Generates the preprocessed .ctx for a source file via ninja and copies it to a .ctx.c
human_pr_text: Generate the preprocessed single-TU source

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Invoke Transmuter with a wrapper compiler command that accepts {{inputPath}} and {{outputPath}}, plus --target, --function, --cwd, and --isolate.

The prepare script claimed --isolate strips non-target, non-inline function bodies and #defines, with claimed faster compiles and fewer iterations in practice; this is PR-authored documentation, not independently verified by this slice.
The demo perturbation changed declaration/call order in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds, producing broken-match evidence suitable as a tool test case but not as a source cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: centiseconds call moved after seconds call

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Matched code decreased by 0.01% / 260 bytes; two broken matches were reported in main/melee/if/iftime: ifTime_UpdateCountdown and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Please resubmit this when it's in a more complete state and with a clearer explanation of its usage.

follow_up_queries: transmuter usage documentation complete state clearer explanation
PRs mentioning Transmuter in doldecomp melee
review comments Transmuter resubmit complete state

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body and reviewer comment
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for all changed files
text_corpus: bot report and issue comments
notes: Changed files and reviewer feedback are well supported by loaded PR evidence.
The PR was closed with merged_at null; no acceptance or merge should be inferred.
The PR was marked draft in raw_pr_json, but the main metadata only reports CLOSED; draft status is supporting detail, not used as an acceptance claim.
No review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
The author explicitly stated the ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds change was a deliberate break for demo purposes, so it should not be promoted as a valid source change.
The prepare script's claimed 30-50% fewer iterations with --isolate is not independently validated in the PR evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Tool demos must separate intentional regressions from accepted decomp progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2419 intentionally broke ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds to demo Transmuter; the bot reported broken matches and the PR was closed after feedback requesting a more complete state and clearer usage explanation. Curate as a tooling-demo caution, not as an accepted source or matching tactic.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider dedicated documentation for Transmuter usage before adding helper scripts
source_update_candidates.text: If Transmuter support is revisited, document the complete workflow: generating a ctx file, copying to .ctx.c, selecting target object, invoking transmuter match with --compiler, --cwd, --function, --target, and explaining isolation/reduction flags. PR #2419 was rejected with feedback requesting a more complete state and clearer usage explanation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/transmuter-prepare.sh prints invocation and tips
issue_comment: clearer explanation of usage requested
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports that usage clarity was a blocker, but it does not establish an accepted project documentation standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Tool-assisted matching PRs should include regression evidence and avoid intentional source breaks except as isolated demos
source_update_candidates.text: Potential standard: when adding or demonstrating a matching-search tool, clearly label any intentional source perturbation, include bot/objdiff/checkdiff evidence, and avoid presenting the perturbation as decomp progress. PR #2419 provides a negative example: an intentional break produced broken-match reports and the PR was closed unmerged.
evidence_refs: pr_body: purposely break function to demo Transmuter
text_corpus: matched code regression and broken matches
source_update_candidates.reason: This aligns with existing verification-ledger principles, but the slice is a single closed demo PR and should be reviewed before becoming a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not promote tools/transmuter-prepare.sh or tools/transmuter-compile.sh as accepted repository tooling from this slice; the PR was closed unmerged and reviewer feedback requested resubmission.
Do not treat the ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds declaration-order change as a valid matching tactic or source cleanup; the author said it was a deliberate break.
Do not promote the script's 30-50% fewer iterations claim as a verified performance result; the slice contains only the script comment, not validation data.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit request for a more complete state and clearer usage explanation.
